title: `podman update` doesn't appear to be able to change memory limits
body:
### Issue Description

```bash
$ sudo podman stats --no-stream | grep spampd
91b51d9d2232  openrc-spampd-2.61              0.45%       171.4MB / 1.074GB  15.96%      0B / 0B     16.77GB / 16.76MB  6           3m41.448282s     0.45%
$ sudo podman update --memory 512m --memory-reservation 256m --memory-swap 512m openrc-spampd-2.61 ; echo $?
91b51d9d223265fbe65595cba2c63a4211efa7c8735acd4b5bd96e44d3925956
0
$ sudo podman stats --no-stream | grep spampd
91b51d9d2232  openrc-spampd-2.61              0.45%       171.4MB / 1.074GB  15.96%      0B / 0B     16.77GB / 16.76MB  6           3m41.448385s     0.45%
```

`podman update` didn't signal any issue in reducing the memory allocation of `91b51d9d2232` from 1GB to 512MB, but the output of `podman stats` `MEM USAGE / LIMIT` doesn't reflect this change.

**Update**: I also checked `podman container inspect` and this also didn't show changed values - so I now suspect that `podman update` is failing and then silently returning successfully rather than `podman stats` being wrong - after the above commands, I can run:

```bash
$ sudo podman container inspect openrc-spampd-2.61 | grep -i mem
                    "--memory-reservation",
                    "--memory",
                    "--memory-swap",
               "Memory": 1073741824,
               "CpusetMems": "",
               "KernelMemory": 0,
               "MemoryReservation": 536870912,
               "MemorySwap": 2147483648,
               "MemorySwappiness": -1,
```

… which doesn't appear to have changed at all.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Start a container with a memory limit and confirm this limit via `podman stats`;
2. Change the memory limit with `podman update` (which outputs the container UID and exits successfully);
3. Despite step (2) succeeding, re-running `podman stats` as-per (1) or running `podman container inspect` shows no change.


### Describe the results you received

`podman update` doesn't appear able to update (reduce?) memory limits.



### Describe the results you expected

`podman update` should action the requested change _or_ indicate some form of failure.

### podman info output

comments.body:
I think that inspect shows no changes is expected for now: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17187

Although I assume podman stats reads from the cgroup so I would think it should show accurate values. Can manually check the limits in the cgroup to see if there were applied?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never heard back, closing.
